simple_types.name: Clock_Id
simple_types.code:
type Clock_Id is
   (GPOUT0, GPOUT1, GPOUT2, GPOUT3, REF, SYS, PERI, USB, …, XOSC);

simple_types.name: GP_Divider

documentation.description: If GP_Divider is 0.0, then it represents (2.0 ** 16)

simple_types.name: VSEL_Volts
simple_types.code:
type VSEL_Volts is
   (VSEL_0_80,
    VSEL_0_85,
    VSEL_0_90,
    VSEL_0_95,
    VSEL_1_00,
    VSEL_1_05,
    VSEL_1_10,
    VSEL_1_15,
    …,
    VSEL_1_30)
with Size => 4;

record_types.name: PLL_Config

documentation.description:
2.18.2. Calculating PLL parameters
PLL = (FREF / REFDIV) * FBDIV / (POSTDIV1 / POSTDIV2)
Common configurations are included below.
Use pico-sdk/src/rp2_common/hardware_clocks/scripts/vcocalc.py

fields.name: FREF
fields.description:
Sets the minimum VREG voltage. If the PLL is reconfigured with a
lower VSEL, the regulator voltage will not decrease

fields.name: REFDIV
fields.description:
Sets the minimum VREG voltage. If the PLL is reconfigured with a
lower VSEL, the regulator voltage will not decrease

fields.name: FBDIV
fields.description:
Sets the minimum VREG voltage. If the PLL is reconfigured with a
lower VSEL, the regulator voltage will not decrease

fields.name: POSTDIV1
fields.description:
Sets the minimum VREG voltage. If the PLL is reconfigured with a
lower VSEL, the regulator voltage will not decrease

fields.name: POSTDIV2
fields.description:
Sets the minimum VREG voltage. If the PLL is reconfigured with a
lower VSEL, the regulator voltage will not decrease

fields.name: VSEL
fields.description:
Sets the minimum VREG voltage. If the PLL is reconfigured with a
lower VSEL, the regulator voltage will not decrease

subtypes.name: Countable_Clock_Id
subtypes.code: subtype Countable_Clock_Id is Clock_Id range REF .. RTC;

documentation.description:
By default, the fractional part of the frequency counter result register
is ignored. Setting Rounded = False includes the fractional frequency,
which may include as much as 2048 KHz of error, depending on the
value of Accuracy. Higher Accuracy values take longer to measure the
clock, but produce more accurate results.

subtypes.name: GP_Output
subtypes.code: subtype GP_Output is Clock_Id range GPOUT0 .. GPOUT3;

subtypes.name: GP_Source
subtypes.code: subtype GP_Source is Clock_Id range REF .. XOSC;

subtypes.name: PLL_Clock_Id
subtypes.code:
subtype PLL_Clock_Id is Clock_Id
   with Static_Predicate => PLL_Clock_Id in PLL_SYS | PLL_USB;

documentation.description:
2.18.2. Calculating PLL parameters
PLL = (FREF / REFDIV) * FBDIV / (POSTDIV1 / POSTDIV2)
Common configurations are included below.
Use pico-sdk/src/rp2_common/hardware_clocks/scripts/vcocalc.py

2.18.2. Calculating PLL parameters
PLL = (FREF / REFDIV) * FBDIV / (POSTDIV1 / POSTDIV2)
Common configurations are included below.
Use pico-sdk/src/rp2_common/hardware_clocks/scripts/vcocalc.py

2.18.2. Calculating PLL parameters
PLL = (FREF / REFDIV) * FBDIV / (POSTDIV1 / POSTDIV2)
Common configurations are included below.
Use pico-sdk/src/rp2_common/hardware_clocks/scripts/vcocalc.py

2.18.2. Calculating PLL parameters
PLL = (FREF / REFDIV) * FBDIV / (POSTDIV1 / POSTDIV2)
Common configurations are included below.
Use pico-sdk/src/rp2_common/hardware_clocks/scripts/vcocalc.py

subtypes.name: SYS_Clock_Id
subtypes.code: subtype SYS_Clock_Id is Clock_Id range PLL_SYS .. XOSC;

subtypes.name: XOSC_Hertz
subtypes.code:
subtype XOSC_Hertz is Hertz range 0 .. 15_000_000
   with Static_Predicate => XOSC_Hertz in 0 | 1_000_000 .. 15_000_000;

documentation.description: The special value 0 indicates that the XOSC is not available.
If GP_Divider is 0.0, then it represents (2.0 ** 16)

constants.name: PLL_125_MHz
constants.code:
PLL_125_MHz : constant PLL_Config :=
   (FREF     => 12_000_000,
    REFDIV   => 1,
    FBDIV    => 125,
    POSTDIV1 => 6,
    POSTDIV2 => 2,
    VSEL     => VSEL_1_10);

constants.name: PLL_133_MHz
constants.code:
PLL_133_MHz : constant PLL_Config :=
   (FREF     => 12_000_000,
    REFDIV   => 1,
    FBDIV    => 133,
    POSTDIV1 => 6,
    POSTDIV2 => 2,
    VSEL     => VSEL_1_10);

constants.name: PLL_200_MHz
constants.code:
PLL_200_MHz : constant PLL_Config :=
   (FREF     => 12_000_000,
    REFDIV   => 1,
    FBDIV    => 100,
    POSTDIV1 => 6,
    POSTDIV2 => 1,
    VSEL     => VSEL_1_15);

constants.name: PLL_250_MHz
constants.code:
PLL_250_MHz : constant PLL_Config :=
   (FREF     => 12_000_000,
    REFDIV   => 1,
    FBDIV    => 125,
    POSTDIV1 => 6,
    POSTDIV2 => 1,
    VSEL     => VSEL_1_15);

constants.name: PLL_48_MHz
constants.code:
PLL_48_MHz : constant PLL_Config :=
   (FREF     => 12_000_000,
    REFDIV   => 1,
    FBDIV    => 64,
    POSTDIV1 => 4,
    POSTDIV2 => 4,
    VSEL     => VSEL_1_10);

documentation.description: Remember to switch clk_sys to another source before modifying PLL_SYS

subprograms.name: Frequency

documentation.description:
By default, the fractional part of the frequency counter result register
is ignored. Setting Rounded = False includes the fractional frequency,
which may include as much as 2048 KHz of error, depending on the
value of Accuracy. Higher Accuracy values take longer to measure the
clock, but produce more accurate results.

subprograms.name: Initialize

documentation.description:
See 2.16.3 Startup Delay for XOSC_Startup_Delay calculation. The default
value is approximately 64ms with a 12 MHz crystal.

The SYS_PLL_Config parameter allows for a custom configuration of the
system PLL that drives the CPU cores and peripherals.
GP will glitch if enabled while changing sources
